author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
śrīvāsera prati, caitanya⋅prasāda,
dekhiyā sakala jana
jaya śrī-caitanya, jaya nityānanda,
bali’ nāce ghana ghana

śrīvāsa⋅mandire, ki bhāva uṭhila,
tāhā ki varṇana haya
bhāvayuddha⋅sane, ānanda⋅krandana,
uṭhe kṛṣṇa⋅premamaya

cāri bhāi paḍi’, prabhura caraṇe,
prema⋅gadagada⋅svare
kā̐diyā kā̐diyā, kākuti kariyā,
gaḍi’ jāya prema-bhare

“ohe prāṇeśvara, e hena vipada,
pratidina jena haya
jāhāte tomāra, caraṇa⋅yugale,
āsakti bāḍite raya

vipada⋅sampade, sei dina bhāla,
je⋅dina tomāre smari
tomāra smaraṇa⋅, rahita je⋅dina,
se⋅dina vipada, hari”

śrīvāsa⋅goṣṭhīra, caraṇe paḍiyā,
bhaktivinoda bhaṇe
tomādera gorā, kṛpā vitariyā,
dekhāo durgata jane
en_translation:
Seeing the mercy shown to Śrīvāsa by Lord Caitanya, all the people repeatedly danced, chanting: "All glories to Śrī Caitanya! All glories to Nityānanda!"

What emotions arose in the temple of Śrīvāsa? How can they ever be described? Amidst a war of ecstasies, the crying of joy, full of love for Kṛṣṇa, arose.

The four brothers fell at the Lord's feet with voices choked with love. Weeping and pleading, they rolled on the ground in great love.

"O Lord of my life! May such distress happen every day, so that my attachment to Your lotus feet may continuously increase.

In distress or prosperity, that day is good when I remember You. The day devoid of Your remembrance—that day is true distress, O Lord!"

Falling at the feet of Śrīvāsa's associates, Bhaktivinoda says: "May your Lord Caitanya, distributing His mercy, reveal Himself to all distressed souls."
